core_meaning: Using 'qui' to introduce a relative clause where the pronoun functions as the subject of the clause.
primary_communicative_fn: Introducing relative clause where pronoun functions as subject of the clause.
when_to_use: Use 'qui' when the relative clause needs a subject and refers to persons or things; 'qui' never changes form.
pattern: Antécédent + qui + verbe (concordance): L'homme qui est venu | Les étudiants qui ont réussi
key_variations: qui + verb (no change): La maison qui s'effondre | qui + pronoun: qui l'a fait (with pronoun object following) | special: c'est...qui (emphasis)
common_learner_mistakes: Adding 'il/elle' after qui; using 'qui' as object when it's for subjects; confusion with 'que' which marks objects.
chunk_compatibility: Foundation for identifying subjects in relative clauses: 'L'homme qui...' provides subject-specific reference.
primary_function: Mark the subject of a relative clause, enabling reference to entities performing actions.
key_forms: qui + indicatif verb: qui parle, qui chante, qui arrive | qui + passé composé: qui a dit, qui a vu
essential_vocabulary: qui, verbe, antécédent, sujet, clause relative
why_it_matters: Essential for building descriptions of people/things performing actions: *L'homme qui parle*, *La raison qui existe*.
common_mistakes: Learners add a subject pronoun after 'qui' (Il homme qui il parle) or confuse it with 'que' for objects.

core_meaning: Using 'que' to introduce a relative clause where the pronoun functions as the direct object.
primary_communicative_fn: Introducing relative clause where pronoun functions as direct object of the clause.
when_to_use: Use 'que' when the relative clause needs a direct object and refers to persons or things; 'que' elides to 'qu'' before a vowel.
pattern: Antécédent + que (+ élision si vowel): L'homme que j'ai vu | La lettre qu'elle a écrite
key_variations: qu' before vowel: la raison qu'il ignore | no change in meaning: Le livre que je lis = Le livre qu'il lit | past participle agreement: la lettre que j'ai écrite (feminine agrees with que)
common_learner_mistakes: Forgetting élision before vowel; not agreeing past participles with preceding direct objects; confusion with 'qui' for subjects.
chunk_compatibility: Describes objects being affected or observed: *La lettre que j'ai reçue*, *L'ami que je connais*.
primary_function: Mark the direct object of a relative clause, enabling reference to entities receiving actions.
key_forms: que + indicatif: que je vois, que tu aimes | que + passé composé: que j'ai vu, qu'il a compris
essential_vocabulary: que, verbe transitif, objet direct, past participle agreement
why_it_matters: Distinguishes who acts (qui) from what receives the action (que): *L'homme qui parle* vs *L'homme que je vois*.
common_mistakes: Not eliding qu' before vowels or forgetting past participle agreement in passé composé with direct objects.

structure_label: Relative pronoun 'qui' after preposition for persons
core_meaning: Using 'qui' after prepositions to refer to persons in relative clauses.
primary_communicative_fn: Using 'qui' after prepositions when the relative clause refers to persons.
when_to_use: Use 'qui' after prepositions (à, de, avec, pour, etc.) when referring to persons; 'qui' is the ONLY form used for persons after prepositions.
pattern: Préposition + qui: L'homme à qui je parle | La femme de qui je dépends | Les enfants avec qui je travaille
key_variations: à qui: à qui appartiennent ces livres? | de qui: de qui parlez-vous? | pour qui: pour qui travaillez-vous? | avec qui: avec qui sortez-vous?
essential_vocabulary_slots: à, de, avec, pour, contre, sans, depuis
common_learner_mistakes: Using 'que' after preposition for persons (incorrect); confusion with English 'whom'; not recognizing preposition + qui pattern.
chunk_compatibility: Identifies people in relationships: *L'ami avec qui je travaille*, *La personne à qui je parle*.
primary_function: Refer to persons who receive actions or are associated via prepositional relationships.
key_forms: à qui, de qui, avec qui, pour qui, contre qui, sans qui, depuis qui
essential_vocabulary: préposition, qui, personne, objet de préposition
why_it_matters: Distinguishes persons after prepositions from objects: *La personne à qui je parle* (person) vs *La chose à laquelle je pense* (thing).
common_mistakes: Using 'que' or 'lequel' instead of 'qui' for persons after prepositions is incorrect.

structure_label: Relative pronoun 'où' for place and time
core_meaning: Using 'où' to refer to locations and temporal references in relative clauses.
primary_communicative_fn: Using 'où' for locations and temporal references in relative clauses.
when_to_use: Use 'où' for places (physical or metaphorical) and for time expressions; 'où' functions as both location and time marker.
pattern: Antécédent (lieu/temps) + où: La ville où je vis | Le jour où nous avons réussi | L'époque où tout a changé
key_variations: lieu: la maison où j'habite | temps: l'année où je suis né | situation: la situation où nous nous trouvons | dans + antecedent + où: dans le cas où
essential_vocabulary_slots: pays, ville, maison, endroit, lieu, jour, an, époque, moment
common_learner_mistakes: Using 'qui' or 'que' for time/location expressions; confusion with 'ou' (or); not recognizing temporal 'où'.
chunk_compatibility: Specifies where or when: *La ville où j'habite*, *Le jour où je suis né*.
primary_function: Indicate location or time in relative clauses without requiring additional prepositions.
key_forms: où + indicatif: où je vis, où il travaille | où + passé composé: où j'ai grandi, où nous avons rencontré
why_it_matters: Avoids prepositional phrases: *Le pays où je viens* (not *de où*) means 'the country where I come from'.
common_mistakes: Using 'que' or 'qui' instead of 'où' for locations or times; confusing 'où' with 'ou' (or).

core_meaning: Using 'dont' when the relative clause contains a 'de' complement (possession, origin, nature).
primary_communicative_fn: Using 'dont' when relative clause needs 'de' complement (possession, origin, nature).
when_to_use: Use 'dont' when the relative clause contains a 'de' complement (of which, about which, from which); 'dont' never changes form.
pattern: Antécédent + dont + suite: La raison dont je parle | L'homme dont je connais le fils | Les pays dont je viens
key_variations: dont + verb: Le livre dont j'ai besoin | dont + noun modified: La femme dont le mari est médecin | dont + de + pronoun: La raison dont je suis fier
essential_vocabulary_slots: avoir besoin de, parler de, dépendre de, se souvenir de, être content de
common_learner_mistakes: Confusing with 'où' for place; using dont when other pronouns needed; word order errors in the subordinate clause.
chunk_compatibility: Expresses belonging or causality: *L'ami dont j'ai besoin*, *La raison dont je suis fier*.
primary_function: Replace 'de + relative antecedent' to indicate possession, origin, or topics of discussion.
key_forms: dont je parle, dont il a besoin, dont elle est fière, dont ils dépendent
why_it_matters: Combines prepositional meaning into one pronoun: *L'ami dont je parle* (= 'the friend of whom I speak').
common_mistakes: Learners confuse dont with où for places or use de qui instead when dont is needed for body-of-noun constructions.

structure_label: Dont for expressions of cause and origin
core_meaning: Using 'dont' with cause, origin, or reason expressions when followed by causal verbs.
primary_communicative_fn: Using 'dont' with cause/origin expressions in relative clauses.
when_to_use: Use 'dont' to express cause, origin, or reason when followed by expressions like 'souffrir de', 'se plaindre de', or causal verbs.
pattern: Antécédent + dont + expression de cause: La raison dont il souffre | Le motif dont il se plaint | L'affaire dont il s'agit
key_variations: dont + de quoi: C'est dont il s'agit | dont + cause: Les problèmes dont il souffre | dont + origin: La famille dont il descend
essential_vocabulary_slots: souffrir de, se plaindre de, dépendre de, mourir de, il s'agit de
common_learner_mistakes: Confusing with 'que' for cause; using in wrong contexts; not recognizing 'dont' replacing 'de + which'.
chunk_compatibility: Explains motivations or causal relationships: *La raison dont il souffre*, *Le problème dont on parle*.
primary_function: Mark the source or reason for an action or state of being in relative clauses.
key_forms: dont + verb (causal): dont il souffre, dont on parle | dont + origin: dont il vient, dont elle descend
essential_vocabulary: dont, cause, origine, souffrir, raison, motif
why_it_matters: Specifies not just what something is, but why or where it comes from: *La situation dont il souffre*.
common_mistakes: Using 'que' to mark cause (incorrect) or overusing dont where simpler constructions would work.

structure_label: Article contractions with relative pronouns after prepositions
core_meaning: Using contracted articles with preposition + relative pronouns in formal French.
primary_communicative_fn: Using contracted articles with preposition + relative for formal style.
when_to_use: Use in formal French when preposition meets a relative pronoun and you want to emphasize the article; common in literary and administrative texts.
pattern: Préposition + article contracté + quel: à + lequel → auquel | de + lequel → duquel | pour + lequel → pour lequel
key_variations: à + lequel → auquel | à + lesquels → auxquels | à + laquelle → à laquelle | de + lequel → duquel | de + lesquels → desquels
essential_vocabulary_slots: à, de, pour, avec, sans, contre, auquel, desquels, desquelles
common_learner_mistakes: Not knowing contracted forms; confusion with simple relative pronouns; not matching gender/number.
chunk_compatibility: Formal emphasis structures: *La raison pour laquelle* (the reason for which), *Les moyens auxquels*.
primary_function: Provide formal, explicit reference to antecedents with prepositions and article emphasis.
key_forms: auquel (à + lequel), duquel (de + lequel), auquel/à laquelle, desquels/desquelles
why_it_matters: Formal written French often uses contracted articles for clarity and stylistic elegance in relative clauses.
common_mistakes: Learners omit articles or use incorrect contractions (au lieu du instead of au auquel).

structure_label: Which/Whom for ambiguous or mixed antecedents
core_meaning: Using 'lequel' when the antecedent is unclear or when clarity requires distinguishing multiple antecedents.
primary_communicative_fn: Using 'lequel' when antecedents are ambiguous or when clarity requires.
when_to_use: Use 'lequel' when the antecedent is unclear, when you need to distinguish between multiple possible antecedents, or after prepositions for things.
pattern: Antécédent + préposition + lequel: La raison pour laquelle | Le moyen par lequel | Les conséquences parmi lesquelles
key_variations: lequel (masc sg) | laquelle (fem sg) | lesquels (masc pl) | lesquelles (fem pl) | contractions: auquel, desquels
essential_vocabulary_slots: pour, par, dans, entre, parmi, sur
common_learner_mistakes: Using qui instead of lequel after prepositions for things; wrong gender/number agreement; overusing when simple form suffices.
chunk_compatibility: Disambiguates references: *La politique pour laquelle il optait* (which policy specifically).
primary_function: Provide gender and number clarity for relative clauses, especially for things after prepositions.
key_forms: lequel/laquelle/lesquels/lesquelles + preposition: pour lequel, parmi lesquels, à laquelle
why_it_matters: Distinguishes between multiple possible antecedents using gender/number agreement.
common_mistakes: Using 'qui' for things after prepositions (qui is only for persons after prepositions).

core_meaning: Using 'quiconque' for universal indefinite reference to any person.
when_to_use: Use 'quiconque' when meaning 'whoever,' 'whoever may be,' 'anyone who' - it implies all possible persons without distinction.
pattern: quiconque + verbe: Quiconque enfreint la loi sera puni | Quiconque le désire peut partir
key_variations: quiconque + indicatif (certain futurity): Quiconque arrive tard | quiconque + subjonctif (hypothetical): Quiconque veuille participer
common_learner_mistakes: Using in informal contexts where it's too formal; confusing with 'qui que ce soit'; spelling errors.
chunk_compatibility: Universal rules and generalizations: *Quiconque arrive tard sera puni* (anyone who arrives late).
primary_function: Express universal, indefinite reference to any person in formal or legal contexts.
key_forms: quiconque + indicatif/subjonctif: Quiconque arrive, Quiconque désire, Quiconque veuille
why_it_matters: Formal legal or regulatory language often requires quiconque for universal applicability.
common_mistakes: Overusing quiconque in casual speech (too formal) or confusing spelling/pronunciation.

core_meaning: Using 'quoi que' with subjunctive mood to express concession about uncertain or hypothetical situations.
primary_communicative_fn: Using 'quoi que' with subjunctive for concession.
when_to_use: Use 'quoi que' (whatever) when conceding something that remains uncertain or hypothetical, followed by subjunctive mood.
pattern: Quoi que + subjonctif: Quoi que vous disiez | Quoi qu'il arrive | Quoi qu'il en soit
key_variations: Quoi qu'il en coûte (whatever the cost) | Quoi qu'il advienne (whatever happens) | Quoi qu'on dise (whatever one says)
essential_vocabulary_slots: dire, faire, arriver, advenir, être, voir, penser
common_learner_mistakes: Using indicative instead of subjunctive; confusion with 'quel que' (which must agree); using for questions.
chunk_compatibility: Expresses resignation or certainty despite unknown outcomes: *Quoi qu'il arrive, je serai là*.
primary_function: Concede uncertainty while expressing determination or constancy regardless of circumstances.
key_forms: Quoi que + subjonctif: Quoi que vous fassiez, Quoi qu'il advienne, Quoi qu'on dise
why_it_matters: Expresses resilience or commitment despite unknowns: 'Whatever you say/do, I will stay'.
common_mistakes: Forgetting subjunctive mood or confusing quoi que with quel que (which must agree with noun).

core_meaning: Using 'quel que' when the antecedent must agree in gender and number with a form of être.
primary_communicative_fn: Using 'quel que' when the antecedent must agree in gender and number.
when_to_use: Use 'quel que' (what/whatever + be) with a form of 'être' when the subject is the thing being described; must agree with subject.
pattern: Quel(le)(s) + que + être + sujet: Quelle que soit la raison | Quels que soient ses motifs | Quelle que puisse être la vérité
key_variations: Quel que soit (masc sg) | Quelle que soit (fem sg) | Quels que soient (masc pl) | Quelles que soient (fem pl)
essential_vocabulary_slots: raison, cause, motif, résultat, conséquence, problème
common_learner_mistakes: Not agreeing gender/number with subject; confusion with 'quoique' (although); using 'quelque' instead.
chunk_compatibility: Emphasizes conditions that remain true regardless: *Quel que soit le problème, nous trouverons une solution*.
primary_function: Acknowledge that despite varying conditions or circumstances, something remains constant.
key_forms: Quel(le)(s) que + soit/soient/puisse être: Quel que soit, Quelle que soit, Quels que soient
essential_vocabulary: quel que, accord, genre, nombre, subjonctif
why_it_matters: Agreement with the subject is mandatory, unlike 'quoi que' which is invariable.
common_mistakes: Forgetting gender/number agreement or using 'quoique' (single word, meaning 'although').

structure_label: The neuter relative pronouns for clauses as antecedents
core_meaning: Using neuter ce + relative when the antecedent is an entire clause or statement, not a specific noun.
primary_communicative_fn: Using neuter ce + relative when the antecedent is an entire clause or statement.
when_to_use: Use 'ce qui', 'ce que', 'ce dont' when the relative clause refers to a whole idea, statement, or situation rather than a specific noun.
pattern: Ce + pronom relatif + verbe: Ce qui m'intéresse | Ce que je veux | Ce dont j'ai besoin | Ce à quoi je pense
key_variations: Ce qui (subject): Ce qui me rassure | Ce que (object): Ce que je vois | Ce dont (de complement): Ce dont il parle
essential_vocabulary_slots: compter, être certain, falloir savoir, s'agir de, penser
common_learner_mistakes: Confusing with 'qui' and 'que' referring to specific nouns; using 'qui' instead of 'que' for object; not recognizing clause-as-antecedent.
chunk_compatibility: Summarizes entire ideas: *Ce qui me plaît*, *Ce dont j'ai besoin*, *Ce que tu dis*.
primary_function: Refer to entire clauses or abstract ideas as antecedents, not individual nouns.
key_forms: Ce qui + verb, Ce que + verb, Ce dont + verb, Ce à quoi + verb
essential_vocabulary: ce, qui, que, dont, à quoi, idée, clause
why_it_matters: Allows relative pronouns to refer to ideas rather than specific nouns: *Ce que je vois* (what I see, not the thing I see).
common_mistakes: Using simple 'qui' or 'que' when 'ce qui/que' is needed for abstract antecedents.

structure_label: Where in the sense of 'whereupon' for result (literary)
core_meaning: Using 'où' to express consequence or result in literary French, meaning 'whereupon' or 'at which point'.
primary_communicative_fn: Using 'où' to express consequence or result in literary French.
when_to_use: Use 'où' in literary/formal French to mean 'where,' 'at which point,' or 'whereby' - expressing consequence or logical progression.
pattern: Clause + où + conséquence: Il a triché, où il fut pris | Cette mesure, où il voyait une avancée
key_variations: où = at which point: L'instant où tout bascula | la manière où (literary): La manière où il procède | rather formal/literary usage
common_learner_mistakes: Using où for reason in modern French; confusion with 'ou' (or); too literary for most contexts.
chunk_compatibility: Narrative progression in literary texts: *Il a refusé, où il se trouva seul*.
primary_function: Express logical consequence or progression in formal or literary narrative.
key_forms: où + indicatif: où il se trouva, où tout bascula, où il vit
why_it_matters: Literary expression of causality without explicit 'donc': 'He refused, whereupon he found himself alone'.
common_mistakes: Using 'où' for reason in modern speech (archaic); confusing with 'ou' (or); overgeneralizing to non-literary contexts.

core_meaning: Using relative pronouns independently when the antecedent is implied, understood, or is a general concept.
primary_communicative_fn: Using relative pronouns independently when antecedent is implied or universal.
when_to_use: Use in formal/literary French where the antecedent is implied, understood, or is a general concept; common in proverbs and formal discourse.
pattern: Qui + verbe (universel): Qui vivra verra (who lives will see) | Que + subj (formal wish): Que chacun fasse son devoir
key_variations: Qui vive (whoever lives) | Qui plus est (moreover - fixed expression) | Que + subjonctif: Vienne la nuit (poetic)
essential_vocabulary_slots: loi, vivre, voir, devoir, faire, réglementation
common_learner_mistakes: Using this construction in informal contexts; confusion about implied antecedent; wrong mood after pronoun.
chunk_compatibility: Proverbs and universal statements: *Qui vivra verra* (time will tell), *Que chacun fasse son devoir*.
primary_function: Express universal truths or formal wishes without explicitly naming the subject.
key_forms: Qui + indicatif: Qui vit, Qui travaille | Que + subjonctif: Que vienne, Que chacun
essential_vocabulary: qui, que, antécédent implicite, universel, proverbe
why_it_matters: Enables fixed expressions and proverbs: *Qui vivra verra* = 'time will tell'.
common_mistakes: Using implicit antecedent patterns in casual conversation (sounds archaic or overly formal).

structure_label: Dont replacing 'de + which' in formal contexts
core_meaning: Using 'dont' in formal writing to replace 'de + which' when elegance or style is desired.
primary_communicative_fn: Formal replacement of 'de + which' with 'dont' for elegance.
when_to_use: Use 'dont' in formal writing to replace 'de + which' when elegance or style is desired; this is especially common in literary and academic French.
pattern: Antécédent + dont + suite de la clause: Les raisons dont (=de lesquelles) il disposait | La situation dont (=de laquelle) il parle
key_variations: dont + de + which: La cause dont (=de laquelle) il s'agit | vs. simple dont: La cause dont il parle
common_learner_mistakes: Using dont when 'de + which' needs different construction; confusion with où for places; overusing in casual speech.
chunk_compatibility: Formal academic writing: *Les théories dont nous parlons*, *Les raisons dont il disposait*.
primary_function: Provide elegant formal reference replacing more cumbersome 'de laquelle/desquels' constructions.
key_forms: dont + verb: dont il s'agit, dont elle dispose | dont replacing de + lequel: dont (=de lequel)
essential_vocabulary: dont, formel, élégance, de laquelle, literary
why_it_matters: Formal French prefers dont over de + lequel for stylistic elegance and readability.
common_mistakes: Overusing dont in casual speech or using it where different prepositional constructions are needed.
